title: epipactis albensis subsp. fibri
intro: Epipactis albensis subsp. fibri, also known as the Icelandic helleborine, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Iceland. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. It has been reported to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues and alleviating respiratory problems, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In terms of herbal preparations, Epipactis albensis subsp. fibri is typically used in tinctures and infusions, although it is essential to note that its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional.
